subject: [PATCH v2 09/21] fuse: handle partial io passthrough for read/write, splice, and mmap

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author agreed to restructure the code in v3 based on reviewer's suggestions for improvement.

Author agrees that the patch looks cleaner and does not address any specific technical concerns or feedback from Amir Goldstein.
reviews.raw_body:
I really like all your suggestions, Amir. I agree that this looks much
nicer. I'll incorporate this into v3.

---

Nice! This looks a lot cleaner.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-05-18.patch_summary: This patch allows servers to pass through only reads or writes instead of both, avoiding cache coherency issues by separating the backing file's page cache from the fuse inode's page cache. It also rejects mmap operations when passthrough is partial or not set for read/writes since mmap requires both read and write access. The patch includes self-contained fallback logic for handling direct io when passthrough is not enabled.
